title: Create a Virtual Machine (Console + gcloud)
aim: To create virtual machines using Google Cloud Console (GUI) and gcloud CLI, and deploy an NGINX web server.

steps.title: Part A: Create VM via Cloud Console (GUI)
steps.description: Create a VM instance using the graphical interface.

steps.title: Open Compute Engine
steps.description: Go to Navigation Menu > Compute Engine > VM Instances. Click Create Instance.

steps.title: Configure the VM
steps.description: Name: gcelab. Region & Zone: assigned in lab. Series: E2. Machine Type: e2-medium (2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM). Boot Disk: Debian GNU/Linux. Firewall: Check Allow HTTP traffic. Click Create.

steps.title: Part B: Install NGINX Web Server
steps.description: Install and verify NGINX on the VM.

steps.title: SSH into the VM
steps.description: In the VM Instances list, click SSH next to gcelab.

steps.title: Install NGINX
steps.description: Run the update and install commands in the SSH terminal.

steps.title: Part C: Create VM via gcloud CLI
steps.description: Use Cloud Shell to create and manage VMs.

steps.title: Activate Cloud Shell
steps.description: Click the Cloud Shell icon (>_) at top right of the console.

steps.title: Set region and zone
steps.description: Run: gcloud config set compute/region REGION, export REGION=REGION, export ZONE=ZONE

steps.title: Create VM with gcloud
steps.description: Run: gcloud compute instances create gcelab2 --machine-type e2-medium --zone=$ZONE

commands.purpose: Check if NGINX process is running

commands.cmd: gcloud compute instances create gcelab2 --machine-type e2-medium --zone=$ZONE

viva.question: What is a VM?
viva.answer: A Virtual Machine (VM) is a compute resource that uses software instead of a physical computer to run programs and deploy apps.

viva.question: How do you SSH into a VM in GCP?
viva.answer: Using the SSH button in the console or 'gcloud compute ssh' command.

notes: Replace REGION and ZONE with the values provided in your lab (e.g., us-east1, us-east1-b).
